votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications, checking for sortedness, lack of duplicates, and exact set intersection equivalence.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications for checking if a list is sorted, has no duplicates, represents the intersection of two lists, and satisfies the combined common_spec.

The Python implementation correctly implements the sorted, no-duplicates, and intersection properties as defined in the Coq specification. The intersection check correctly verifies both directions of the logical equivalence.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs. is_sorted checks adjacent elements which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists. no_duplicates checks for uniqueness like NoDup. is_intersection checks both directions of the iff: that every element in result is in both l1 and l2, and that every element in both l1 and l2 is in result. common_spec combines all three conditions with conjunction. All functions return the same boolean judgments as the Coq predicates would evaluate to on concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs: is_sorted checks adjacent elements which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists; no_duplicates checks for repeated elements; is_intersection verifies the bidirectional membership condition (result contains only elements in both l1 and l2, and all common elements are in result); common_spec combines all three checks. The Python implementations will return the same boolean judgments as the Coq predicates for any concrete list inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs: is_sorted checks adjacent elements which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists; no_duplicates checks for repeated elements matching NoDup; is_intersection checks the biconditional by verifying all result elements are in both l1 and l2, and all elements in both l1 and l2 appear in result; common_spec combines all three conditions with conjunction.

Python checks non-decreasing adjacent pairs (equivalent to Coq’s pairwise Z.le), enforces NoDup via membership, and its is_intersection ensures exactly the set of elements common to both lists (subset and superset directions). Conjunction matches Coq’s common_spec on all concrete integer lists.

is_sorted checks non-decreasing order (equivalent to Coq’s all-pairs condition), no_duplicates matches NoDup, and is_intersection ensures result equals the set intersection of l1 and l2. Thus common_spec matches the Coq spec on all inputs.
Python enforces result equals the set intersection (both directions), no duplicates, and non-decreasing order; these align with the Coq specs over integer lists.
